mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and driving positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, consumer spending power deteriorates significantly, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures combined with a declining federal funds rate amplify liquidity-driven asset repricing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as consumer demand meets cheap credit.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observed periods, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals peak corporate capacity utilization and margin expansion, triggering short-term momentum buying in the S&P 500 over the next week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, mechanically supporting higher S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon through fundamental earnings growth.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year sample, with Spearman rho below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy and erode real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market and construction sector recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through broad-based economic expansion.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of quarters, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production amid sticky inflation signals supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO contracts during periods of CPIAUCSL growth above 2.5% annually.
